# PO PDF — Add Approval Notes Section — Specification

## Current State Analysis

The PO PDF is generated by `backend/src/services/pdf.service.ts` (`generatePurchaseOrderPdf`, pdfkit-based) and assembled/queried by `backend/src/services/purchaseOrder.service.ts:generatePOPdf` (lines 1547-1584), called from `backend/src/controllers/purchaseOrder.controller.ts:412-429` via `GET /api/purchase-orders/:id/pdf`. Frontend triggers this as a plain blob download (`purchaseOrder.service.ts:downloadPdf` → `useDownloadPOPdf`) with zero PDF-content awareness — this is a **backend-only** change.

`generatePOPdf` already queries `statusHistory` filtered to the three approval stages and orders it ascending by `changedAt` (`purchaseOrder.service.ts:1555-1559`):
```ts
statusHistory: {
  where: { toStatus: { in: ['supervisor_approved', 'finance_director_approved', 'dos_approved'] } },
  include: { changedBy: { select: { firstName: true, lastName: true } } },
  orderBy: { changedAt: 'asc' },
},
```
Because this `include` has no `select`, Prisma already returns every scalar column on each `RequisitionStatusHistory` row — **`notes` is already present on `po.statusHistory[i].notes`**, just not read. `findApproval` (lines 1566-1573) currently extracts only `name`/`date` into `supervisorApproval`/`financeApproval`/`dosApproval`, which are consumed by `pdf.service.ts`'s signature-block renderer (`drawSigBlock`, lines 334-362) to show the cursive approver name + date above each signature line. `po.notes` (general/creation-time notes, unrelated to approval notes) already has its own "ADDITIONAL INFORMATION" section (`pdf.service.ts:313-318`) rendered full-width, right before the signature lines — this is the styling template to mirror.

## Problem Definition

Add the per-stage approval notes (already shown on the web PO detail page's Status Timeline and Notes section, per the two prior features) to the generated PDF as well, so a printed/downloaded PO carries the same approver commentary.

## Proposed Solution Architecture

Two files change, no schema/query changes (data is already fetched):

### 1. `backend/src/services/purchaseOrder.service.ts`
Extend `findApproval` (lines 1566-1573) to also carry `notes`:
```ts
const findApproval = (toStatus: string) => {
  const entry = (po.statusHistory as any[]).find((h: any) => h.toStatus === toStatus);
  if (!entry) return null;
  return {
    name:  `${entry.changedBy.firstName} ${entry.changedBy.lastName}`,
    date:  entry.changedAt as Date,
    notes: (entry.notes as string | null) ?? null,
  };
};
```
No other change needed in this file — `poWithApprovals` already spreads the result of `findApproval` into `supervisorApproval`/`financeApproval`/`dosApproval`.

### 2. `backend/src/services/pdf.service.ts`
- Extend the `POForPdf` interface (lines 63-66) so each approval carries the new optional `notes` field:
  ```ts
  supervisorApproval?: { name: string; date: Date; notes?: string | null } | null;
  financeApproval?:    { name: string; date: Date; notes?: string | null } | null;
  dosApproval?:        { name: string; date: Date; notes?: string | null } | null;
  ```
- Insert a new **"APPROVAL NOTES"** section between the existing "Additional Information" block (ends line 318, after its `hRule`/`moveDown`) and the "Signature Lines" block (starts line 320), mirroring the "ADDITIONAL INFORMATION" section's bold-header/regular-body style (`FONT_BLD`/10pt/`PRIMARY` header, `FONT_REG`/9pt/`#212121` body), but only rendered when at least one approval stage actually has a note (unlike "Additional Information", which always renders with an `N/A` fallback — an "always visible, mostly empty" block for 3 possible approvers would add noise to the common case where nobody left a comment):
  ```ts
  // ---- Approval Notes (only rendered when at least one stage has a note) ---
  const approvalNotesList = [
    { label: 'Supervisor',          approval: po.supervisorApproval },
    { label: 'Finance Director',    approval: po.financeApproval },
    { label: 'Director of Schools', approval: po.dosApproval },
  ].filter((e) => e.approval?.notes);

  if (approvalNotesList.length > 0) {
    doc.font(FONT_BLD).fontSize(10).fillColor(PRIMARY)
      .text('APPROVAL NOTES', MARGIN, doc.y, { align: 'left', width: COL_W });
    doc.moveDown(0.2);
    for (const { label, approval } of approvalNotesList) {
      const dateStr = approval!.date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { year: 'numeric', month: 'short', day: 'numeric' });
      doc.font(FONT_BLD).fontSize(9).fillColor('#212121')
        .text(`${label} (${approval!.name}, ${dateStr}):`, MARGIN, doc.y, { width: COL_W });
      doc.font(FONT_REG).fontSize(9).fillColor('#212121')
        .text(approval!.notes as string, MARGIN, doc.y, { width: COL_W });
      doc.moveDown(0.3);
    }
    doc.moveDown(0.2);
    hRule(doc, doc.y);
    doc.moveDown(0.5);
  }
  ```
  Placed immediately after line 318 (`doc.moveDown(0.5);` following "Additional Information"'s `hRule`) and before line 320's `doc.moveDown(1.5);` (start of Signature Lines).

## Implementation Steps

1. Extend `findApproval` in `purchaseOrder.service.ts` to include `notes`.
2. Extend `POForPdf` interface in `pdf.service.ts` (3 fields) to accept `notes?: string | null`.
3. Insert the new "APPROVAL NOTES" conditional block in `pdf.service.ts` between the "Additional Information" and "Signature Lines" sections.
4. No route/controller/frontend changes.

## Dependencies

None new — same `pdfkit` already in use.

## Configuration Changes

None.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Long approval notes (up to 1000 chars per `ApproveSchema.notes`) pushing the Signature Lines block off the bottom of the page, since pdfkit auto-paginates via `doc.text()` height overflow but the Signature block uses fixed `sigTopY`/`lineY` offsets computed from `doc.y` *before* checking for a new page. **Mitigation:** this exact risk already exists today for `po.notes` (which is capped at 2000 chars) in the "Additional Information" block placed in the same position — the approval-notes block adds no new class of risk, just more of the same, and is out of scope for this task to fix pagination overflow behavior that pre-dates it.
- **Risk:** `po.statusHistory` on the Prisma-generated type doesn't declare `notes` explicitly in TS since the `include` has no `select` — relying on Prisma's default "all scalar fields" behavior. **Mitigation:** confirmed directly by reading the `RequisitionStatusHistory` Prisma model (`notes String?` is a plain scalar column, not a relation), so Prisma's generated type for this include already includes `notes: string | null` — no query change needed, and this was already true before this task (the field was simply unread).

## Files to Modify

- `backend/src/services/purchaseOrder.service.ts`
- `backend/src/services/pdf.service.ts`
